tests.title: Transparent memory store: private file, keyword recall, fail-soft (#59)
tests.requires: This is the store/retrieval FOUNDATION (#59) — there is no voice surface yet (recall wiring is #61). Verify at the file + Python-shell level. Default `[memory]` config; embedding backend OFF.
tests.expected: The memory file lives under the writable data dir, is git-ignored (private, never leaves the machine), loads fail-soft skipping a corrupt line, and keyword/tag recall returns the most relevant facts offline with no new dependency.

steps.text: Hand-create the memory file (`memory.jsonl` under the `[memory].dir` data dir) with a few one-fact-per-line entries, e.g. `{"text":"prefers the Krait Mk II for combat","tags":["ship"]}`.
Confirm it's git-ignored: `git status` does NOT list the memory file (private data never committed).
Fail-soft: add a deliberately broken line (e.g. `not json`) → loading skips just that line, keeps the good facts, and doesn't crash.

Keyword recall offline: in a Python shell, load the store and run a `Retriever(...).recall("which ship for combat")` → the Krait fact ranks top; tag filter works; no network, no new dependency (embedding backend stays off).

tests.title: Persistent memory - automatic capture: journal milestones + piggybacked facts, dedup, cap (issue #60)
tests.requires: `[memory].enabled = true` (default) and, for milestones, `[elite].enabled = true`. Memory lives at `<data dir>/memory/memory.jsonl` (git-ignored).
tests.expected: Launch logs 'Persistent memory ON (capture + recall).'; a notable journal event writes a milestone line with NO model call; 'remember that...' adds a preference/note line in the SAME reply (one turn, not two); an exact repeat is deduped; a low cap drops oldest milestones first while explicit facts survive; a relaunch doesn't re-capture old milestones; the memory capability is listed in help.

steps.text: On launch the log shows `Persistent memory ON (capture + recall).`

Journal milestone (deterministic, no cost): in-game, jump to an unexplored system and detailed-scan a first-discovery body, or fully map a body. A new line appears in `memory/memory.jsonl` (`First to discover ...` / `Fully mapped ...`, type `milestone`). No LLM/router/usage line accompanies it - capture is a local write.

Conversation fact (piggybacked, no extra call): say 'remember that I prefer the Krait Mk II'. COVAS acknowledges in-character in the SAME reply; a preference/note line is added. Confirm the router logged ONE turn, not two (no extra model call).
Dedup: repeat the exact same 'remember that...' -> it is NOT added a second time (log: `Already knew that...`), and the file still has one copy.

Cap: set `[memory].cap` low (e.g. 3), add a couple of 'remember that...' facts, then generate several milestones. The file stays at the cap; the oldest milestones drop first while your explicitly-remembered facts survive.
Relaunch COVAS++ with the same journal present -> old milestones are NOT re-captured (capture only sees live events; startup priming doesn't republish).
Ask 'what can you do' -> the memory capability is listed; drilling in mentions remembering facts and milestones.

tests.title: Persistent memory - recall in conversation: auto-injection, silent no-match, wake word, cache-safe (issue #61)
tests.requires: `[memory].enabled = true` (default). Seed a fact first: say 'remember that my main ship is a Krait Mk II' (or hand-add a line to `memory/memory.jsonl`). Recall is keyword/tag, offline and free (no router/usage line for the recall block).
tests.expected: A past-referencing question is answered from the stored fact (a `memory-recall` log line shows the matched reason); a no-match says it isn't on file and injects nothing; an unrelated turn fires no recall; 'recall ...' forces a lookup with the word scrubbed; an explicit 'what do you know about...' may call the recall_memory tool; across recall turns the cached-prompt token count doesn't climb (the block rides the current user message only).

steps.text: Automatic recall injection: ask 'do you remember my main ship?' -> COVAS answers from the stored fact ('a Krait Mk II'), not a guess. The log shows a `memory-recall` line with the matched reason.

No-match is silent: ask 'do you remember my favourite music?' (nothing stored) -> COVAS says it doesn't have that on file; the log's `memory-recall` note reads `(no matching memory)` and nothing is injected.
Plain turn untouched: ask an unrelated question ('tell me a joke') -> no `memory-recall` line, no memory block - recall only fires on past-referencing turns.
Wake-word override: say 'recall, what's my main ship' -> forces a lookup; the word 'recall' is scrubbed from what COVAS answers about (it doesn't echo it back).
Explicit tool path: ask 'what do you know about my ship?' -> COVAS may call the `recall_memory` tool and reports the stored fact; a miss returns 'nothing on file'.

Cache-safe (no prefix growth): across several recall turns, replies stay quick and the cached-prompt token count doesn't climb turn-over-turn - the memory block rides the current user message only, never the cached system prompt.

tests.title: Memory browser tab: list, add, search, edit (lossless), delete, voice sync, stale-write guard (issue #62)
tests.requires: Panel open at http://127.0.0.1:8765/memory. Reads/writes the SAME `memory/memory.jsonl` the voice loop uses. `[memory].enabled = true` (default). Pure vanilla JS - no CDN, works offline. Use THROWAWAY facts.
tests.expected: The memory tab lists every memory with type/tags/timestamp; ADD writes a new JSON line (heard live by voice); search filters live with an N/total count; EDIT round-trips losslessly (id + original when unchanged); DELETE removes just that line; a voice capture appears after RELOAD; a stale-write guard shows an amber warning and refuses to clobber, with a Live-Log note on a successful web write.

steps.text: Tab + nav: the memory link exists on the control-panel, settings, and checklist headers; opening it lists EVERY memory with type, tags, and timestamp; the header shows the file name.

Add: type a fact (e.g. 'prefers metric units'), pick a type, add a tag -> ADD -> a new JSON line appears in `memory/memory.jsonl`; ask by voice 'do you remember what units I use?' -> COVAS answers from the new fact.
Search: type in the search box -> the list filters live by text, tag, or type; the count shows `N / total`; clearing restores all.
Edit: click EDIT, change text/type/tags -> SAVE -> the file line updates; the memory's `id` and original `when` are unchanged (round-trips losslessly), only the edited fields differ.
Delete: click DELETE on a throwaway memory, confirm -> that line is gone from the file; the rest survive.
Voice -> web: say 'remember that my callsign is Ghost', then click RELOAD FROM DISK (or refocus) -> the captured fact appears in the list.

Stale-write guard: load the tab, make a voice memory so the file changes, then ADD/EDIT/DELETE in the tab -> an amber warning appears and the write is refused; RELOAD pulls in the voice memory. The Live Log (All) shows 'Memory updated from the web browser' on a successful web write.
